quoteFirst: While people were yet wondering how the _Sirius_ so successfully made out to cross the rude Atlantic, it was announced
quoteLast: on Monday, from the telegraph, that a huge steamship was in the offing.
title: History of merchant shipping and ancient commerce, Volume 4 (of 4)
author: Lindsay, W. S. (William Schaw), 1816-1877

title:
The Cruise of the Alabama and the Sumter

quoteFirst: Aboard a ship where there were a lot of young men passengers, and jumping back and forth over open hatches, diving from the yardarm, catching sharks, and revolver practice at men-of-war hawks, molly hawks, cape pigeons, catching albatross with a hook and line, etc., were among the amusements, some of us met at
quoteLast: to breakfast in a cabin the owner of which had a hamper of cakes and two boxes of Partaga and Regalia Brittanica cigars, these men amongst whom were a T-- and two M's--had been brought up on civilized things so the unfortunate owner's cigars went fast.
title: A Tramp's Scraps
author: Self, H. I. M.

quoteTime: About eleven o’clock in the morning
quoteLast: we found our way blocked by a small detachment of the enemy which had been left to make a stand at an isolated _khan_, one of those walled desert halting-places of the caravanserai order,--really more of a fort than a tavern.
title: Many Fronts
author: Freeman, Lewis R. (Lewis Ransome), 1878-1960

quoteFirst: At the time of low water,
quoteLast: , the columns for the assault moved out of the trenches by the openings in front of the battery, and in a few minutes after the advance of the forlorn hope, the enemy exploded two mines, which blew down part of the sea-line wall; but as the troops were not in very close order, nor very near the wall, their loss was not great.
title: The Great Sieges of History
author: Robson, William, 1785-1863; Gilbert, John, 1817-1897 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: , and on arrival in New York to get our lunch before keeping the appointments, which were usually made for two o'clock.

quoteFirst: On the 3d of May, the weather being fine, though interrupted occasionally by a rain-squall, we gave chase,
quoteLast: , to a clipper-ship, with square yards, white canvas, and long mast-heads--and the reader must be enough of an expert, by this time, to know what these mean.
title: Memoirs of Service Afloat, During the War Between the States
